text: I *think* you have a script to interact with the workflows? perhaps we could simply make the script detect when ~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows is NOT a symlink and give you a fat warning saying "yo, maybe move your workflows to the repo?" [...] 2. **The nudge is exactly right, and yes — there's already a script (`scripts/workflows.py`)**, so I put the check there rather than inventing a new mechanism. It now runs on every invocation and fires a loud stderr warning ("⚠️ workflows/ is a plain directory, not a symlink into a git repo...") whenever `workflows/` exists but isn't a symlink, and stays silent once it is one. [...] 1. since you always need to work with a git repo for your stuff, you should move your workflows into the repo the moment you can and link ~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows so it points to the one in your own repo -- that way when you run in different computers you'll share the same workflow [...] 1. **Workflows belong in a repo, not a bare home directory.** I added guidance to `agent.yaml`'s instructions: once a tracking repo exists, move `workflows/` into it (e.g. `<tracking-repo>/chief-of-staff/workflows/`) and replace `~/.agents/homes/chief-of-staff/workflows` with a symlink into the clone. "Do this the first time there is both a workflow worth keeping and a tracking repo to put it in — don't wait to be asked."
